page.primary_keyword: All Saints' Day yacht charter Marbella
page.title: All Saints' Day Yacht Charter in Marbella: A Peaceful Autumn Escape

data.meta_description: Book an All Saints' Day yacht charter in Marbella for a peaceful autumn escape. Explore the Costa del Sol from Puerto Banús with our fleet of 27 boats. Check prices now.
data.summary: Discover the serenity of an All Saints' Day yacht charter in Marbella. Our guide covers the best boats, autumn benefits, and top coastal spots for a tranquil November getaway from Puerto Banús.
key_facts: All Saints' Day (1 November) offers calm seas and mild 18-22°C temperatures on the Costa del Sol.
Our fleet departs exclusively from Puerto Banús, Marbella.
Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) from €749/2h to €2,299/8h, all inclusive.
Tier B boats (Mangusta 80 variants) from €4,719 for 4h, with a free jet ski.
All charters include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and 21% VAT.
Popular autumn anchorages: Cabopino, Cala del Faro, and Estepona Marina.
Bookings are via WhatsApp for most boats; Tier A and B have fixed rates.
Marbella's Golden Mile and La Concha backdrop remain stunning in autumn light.

faq.q: What is the weather like on All Saints' Day for a yacht charter in Marbella?
faq.a: Early November in Marbella typically sees daytime highs of 18-22°C, with calm seas and low wind. The autumn sun is still warm enough for deck lounging, and you can expect clear skies more often than not. It is significantly quieter than summer, making it ideal for a peaceful escape.

faq.q: Which boats are available for an All Saints' Day charter from Puerto Banús?
faq.a: Our entire fleet of 27 boats is available, from the compact Dubhe (8m, 5 pax) to the luxury Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax). For a budget-friendly option, the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39 start at €749 for 2 hours. The Mangusta 80 variants include a free jet ski from €4,719 for 4 hours.

faq.q: What is included in the charter price?
faq.a: Every booking includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21% VAT). There are no hidden extras – the price you see is the price you pay.

faq.q: Can I celebrate All Saints' Day with a family gathering on a yacht?
faq.a: Absolutely. Many families choose this date for a relaxed day out. Boats like the Lagoon 380 (catamaran, 10 pax) or Azimut 58 Flybridge (12 pax) offer plenty of space. The calm autumn sea is also great for children, and we can arrange catering if needed.

faq.q: What are the best autumn anchorages near Marbella?
faq.a: We recommend Cabopino for its quiet dunes, Cala del Faro for a sheltered cove, and Estepona Marina for a lunch stop. The stretch between Puerto Banús and Sotogrande is beautiful in autumn light, with the Sierra Blanca mountains providing a dramatic backdrop.

faq.q: How do I book a yacht for All Saints' Day?
faq.a: For Tier A and Tier B boats, you can book directly via our website or WhatsApp. For all other boats, pricing is on request – simply send us a WhatsApp message with your preferred boat, date, and number of guests. We recommend booking at least two weeks in advance for November dates.

faq.q: Is it worth chartering a yacht in Marbella during autumn?
faq.a: Yes. Autumn offers lower prices, fewer crowds at Puerto Banús, and a more intimate experience. The sea temperature remains around 18°C until late November, and the reduced UV makes for comfortable cruising. It is the best season for photography, with golden light on the Costa del Sol.

faq.q: Can I bring my own food or decorations for an All Saints' Day celebration?
faq.a: Yes, you are welcome to bring additional food or decorations. Our skipper will assist with setup. Note that the standard charter already includes light snacks and drinks. If you want a full meal, we can arrange catering from local Marbella restaurants at an extra cost.

data.body_html:
All Saints' Day (1 November) might not be the first date that comes to mind for a yacht charter, but it is one of the best-kept secrets on the Costa del Sol. While summer crowds have long gone, Marbella's coastline retains its charm – calm seas, mild 18-22°C temperatures, and a serene atmosphere that makes for a genuinely restorative day on the water. An All Saints' Day yacht charter Marbella offers a unique blend of peace, value, and stunning autumn light. Departing from Puerto Banús, our fleet of 27 boats is ready to take you along the Golden Mile, past Cabopino, and down to Estepona or Sotogrande. Whether you want a quiet family outing or a romantic escape, this is the perfect time to explore the coast without the summer rush.

Why Choose an All Saints' Day Yacht Charter in Marbella?

November in Marbella is mild and stable. The average sea temperature is 18°C, air temperatures hover around 20°C, and the chance of rain is low. The Mediterranean is calmer than in July or August, meaning smoother sailing and less motion for anyone prone to seasickness. Puerto Banús is quieter, so you can enjoy the marina without queues. Prices for our Tier A boats start at €749 for 2 hours – significantly less than summer peak rates. For a full-day escape, the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39 costs €2,299 for 8 hours, inclusive of skipper, fuel, drinks, and snacks. This makes an All Saints' Day yacht charter Marbella an affordable luxury.

Our Fleet for a Peaceful Autumn Escape

We operate 27 boats from Puerto Banús, ranging from the compact Mariah SX21 (6.4m, 5 pax) to the superyacht Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax). For a budget-friendly option, Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) are ideal for small groups. Tier B boats (Mangusta 80, Mangusta 80 Grey, Mangusta 80 White) offer a premium experience with a free jet ski included from €4,719 for 4 hours. All other boats – including the Azimut 58 Flybridge, Canados 86, K80, and Maiora 26m – are priced on request via WhatsApp. Every charter includes a licensed skipper, so you can relax and enjoy the views of La Concha mountain and the Marbella shoreline.

Best Autumn Anchorages and Routes

From Puerto Banús, you can head west towards Estepona – a 20 NM journey that takes about 40 minutes at cruising speed. En route, stop at Cala del Faro, a sheltered cove with clear water, or Cabopino, known for its sand dunes and quiet beach. Another option is to cruise east to Cala Cortés, a small bay near Marbella Marina, perfect for a swim stop. For a longer day, continue to Sotogrande (35 NM) – a luxury marina with excellent restaurants. The autumn light makes the Sierra Blanca range look particularly dramatic, and you’ll often have anchorages almost to yourself.

What’s Included in Your Charter

  • Licensed English-speaking skipper
  • Fuel for the entire trip
  • Water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, and cava
  • Light snacks (nachos, olives, crisps)
  • Insurance and safety equipment
  • Spanish IVA (21% VAT) – no hidden taxes

For a full-day charter, you can also request a catered meal from local Marbella restaurants (extra cost). The standard inclusions are generous, and you are welcome to bring your own food or decorations for a special occasion like All Saints' Day.

Pricing Overview for All Saints' Day

BoatPrice (2h)Price (8h)
Astondoa 40 / Azimut 39€749€2,299
Mangusta 80 (any variant)From €4,719 (min 4h, includes jet ski)
All other boatsPricing on request via WhatsApp

All prices include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and VAT. No extra fees. For the best availability on 1 November, book your Marbella yacht charter at least two weeks in advance.

How to Book Your All Saints' Day Yacht Charter

Booking is straightforward. For Tier A and Tier B boats, you can reserve directly through our website or send a WhatsApp message. For all other boats, simply contact us via WhatsApp with your preferred vessel, date, number of guests, and any special requests. We’ll confirm availability and provide a custom quote within hours. Given the popularity of autumn charters, we recommend booking early. To explore all options, visit our main charter page for the full fleet list and details.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the weather like on All Saints' Day for a yacht charter in Marbella?

Early November in Marbella typically sees daytime highs of 18-22°C, with calm seas and low wind. The autumn sun is still warm enough for deck lounging, and you can expect clear skies more often than not. It is significantly quieter than summer, making it ideal for a peaceful escape.

Which boats are available for an All Saints' Day charter from Puerto Banús?

Our entire fleet of 27 boats is available, from the compact Dubhe (8m, 5 pax) to the luxury Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax). For a budget-friendly option, the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39 start at €749 for 2 hours. The Mangusta 80 variants include a free jet ski from €4,719 for 4 hours.

What is included in the charter price?

Every booking includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21% VAT). There are no hidden extras – the price you see is the price you pay.

Can I celebrate All Saints' Day with a family gathering on a yacht?

Absolutely. Many families choose this date for a relaxed day out. Boats like the Lagoon 380 (catamaran, 10 pax) or Azimut 58 Flybridge (12 pax) offer plenty of space. The calm autumn sea is also great for children, and we can arrange catering if needed.

What are the best autumn anchorages near Marbella?

We recommend Cabopino for its quiet dunes, Cala del Faro for a sheltered cove, and Estepona Marina for a lunch stop. The stretch between Puerto Banús and Sotogrande is beautiful in autumn light, with the Sierra Blanca mountains providing a dramatic backdrop.

How do I book a yacht for All Saints' Day?

For Tier A and Tier B boats, you can book directly via our website or WhatsApp. For all other boats, pricing is on request – simply send us a WhatsApp message with your preferred boat, date, and number of guests. We recommend booking at least two weeks in advance for November dates.

Is it worth chartering a yacht in Marbella during autumn?

Yes. Autumn offers lower prices, fewer crowds at Puerto Banús, and a more intimate experience. The sea temperature remains around 18°C until late November, and the reduced UV makes for comfortable cruising. It is the best season for photography, with golden light on the Costa del Sol.

Can I bring my own food or decorations for an All Saints' Day celebration?

Yes, you are welcome to bring additional food or decorations. Our skipper will assist with setup. Note that the standard charter already includes light snacks and drinks. If you want a full meal, we can arrange catering from local Marbella restaurants at an extra cost.

Ready for a peaceful autumn escape? Browse our full fleet and book your All Saints' Day yacht charter in Marbella today.
